channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Rescue crews working through the night pulled two women from rubble more than seven hours after a Russian missile struck a private medical clinic in a southern Ukraine city, killing six people and wounding 22 others, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law delcaration, officials said Wednesday, as President Yoon Suk Yeol’s office resisted a police attempt to search the compound.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 29, Palestinian medics say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip killed at least 29 people overnight and into Wednesday, including one that hit a home where displaced people were sheltering in the isolated north, killing 19, according to Palestinian medical officials.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A New Zealander studied for a year to win the Spanish world Scrabble title. He doesn’t speak Spanish
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A New Zealand man playing his first-ever competitive Scrabble game in Spanish, a language he doesn’t speak, has won the board game’s Spanish-language world title — despite the language barrier that stopped him from chatting with his opponents.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike on home in northern Gaza kills 19 people, Palestinian medics say
item.description: Palestinian medical officials say an Israeli strike on a home where displaced people were sheltering in the northern Gaza Strip has killed at least 19 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Geminids meteor shower peaks this weekend: How to watch
item.description: People across the globe could see up to 120 meteors per hour during the peak of the Geminids meteor shower this week.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, KTLA 5

item.title: Alex Jones keeps Infowars for now after judge rejects The Onion's winning auction bid
item.description: A federal judge in Texas rejected the auction sale of Alex Jones’ Infowars to The Onion satirical news outlet, criticizing the bidding for the conspiracy theory platform as flawed as well as how much money families of the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary shooting stood to receive.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Atmospheric river and potential bomb cyclone bring chaotic winter weather to East Coast
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast was beginning a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather on Wednesday that was rainy, windy and potentially dangerous, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrities and coastal residents flee from wind-driven wildfire in Malibu
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Evacuation orders and warnings have gone out to 20,000 Southern California residents Tuesday as firefighters battled a wind-driven wildfire in Malibu that burned near celebrities' seaside mansions, horse farms and Pepperdine University, the sheriff's department said.
item.dc:creator: JAE C. HONG, ERIC THAYER and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mega Millions winner sues for other half of $394M jackpot, claims he bought both tickets
item.description: A man is suing the California Lottery, claiming he bought both winning tickets for a $394 million Mega Millions jackpot but lost one and is owed the remaining $197 million.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KREX 5

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled to Eastern Europe is in custody, online records show
item.description: Online records show a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left behind a wife and three children to go to Eastern Europe is in a Wisconsin jail.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Proposed merger of supermarket giants Kroger and Albertsons is halted by federal, state judges
item.description: The proposed merger between supermarket giants Kroger and Albertsons floundered on Tuesday after judges overseeing two separate cases both halted the deal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Joe Burrow's home broken into during Monday Night Football
item.description: Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into during Monday Night Football in the latest home invasion of a pro athlete in the U.S., authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: After acquittal in subway chokehold trial, Daniel Penny says he was vulnerable in the encounter
item.description: The Marine veteran who choked a volatile, mentally ill man on a New York subway says he put himself in a “very vulnerable position.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The 26-year-old man charged in last week’s killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO appeared in a Pennsylvania courtroom Tuesday, where he was denied bail and his lawyer said he'd fight extradition to New York City, where the attack happened. Luigi Nicholas Mangione was arrested Monday in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: FBI says 2 Homeland Security agents in Utah sold illegal drugs for profit through informant
item.description: Federal authorities say that two Department of Homeland Security agents in Utah illegally sold drugs through an informant in a scheme that brought in up to $300,000.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Making a $1B investment in the US? Trump pledges expedited permits — but there are hurdles
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is promising expedited federal permits for energy projects and other construction worth more than $1 billion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: When fire threatened a California university, the school says it knew what to do
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — The wildfire alert came in the middle of the night as some college students in Southern California were cramming for final exams and others were woken up in their dorms.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN and ERIC THAYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: James Comer predicts Biden will pardon his brother Jim
item.description: “I do think he’ll pardon other family members. He’s going to have to,” Comer said in the clip highlighted by Grabien.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Belichick's known ties to the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
item.description: Belichick, 72, is considered by many to be the greatest NFL head coach of all time and was the architect of the New England Patriots dynasty from 2001-2019.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Ruffalo presses Biden to act fast on 'forever chemicals' before Trump takes office
item.description: "The EPA has worked their butts off, against all odds, to get a drinking water standard on this particular chemical class," Ruffalo said.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Greene says she is safe after bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) confirmed she is safe after reports swirled of an email sent to law enforcement containing a bomb threat targeting the lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: FTC issuing refunds to Fortnite gamers after unwanted charges
item.description: According to the FTC, the company unlawfully charged players for unwanted purchases, let children rack up unauthorized charges without their parents’ permission, and blocked some users who disputed wrongful charges.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, KRON4

item.title: Top 12 holiday scams to watch out for, according to the BBB
item.description: The BBB has released a list of the top 12 holiday season scams, including Amazon Imposters, misleading social media ads, AI scams, gift exchange schemes, holiday apps, fake texts, gift card offers, temporary holiday jobs, look-alike websites, package delivery, porch pirates, and puppy scams.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, has promised to deliver a massive reform of a bureau he sees as among the agencies that have “weaponized the government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Family of suspect speaks out after his arrest in UnitedHealthcare CEO's murder
item.description: The family of Luigi Mangione, the 26-year-old man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City, has released a statement following his arrest on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO struggles, shouts while entering courthouse
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO struggled with deputies and shouted while arriving for a court appearance in Pennsylvania a day after he was arrested at a McDonald’s and charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 6 Guatemalans arrested and charged with human smuggling in deadly 2021 Mexico truck crash
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Six Guatemalans were arrested in Guatemala and in Texas Monday on human smuggling charges linked to a 2021 semitrailer truck crash in Mexico that killed more than 50 migrants, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Local Pennsylvania officer hailed hero for 'swift' actions in arrest of UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro praised Altoona Police Department Office Tyler Frye for his swift actions in arresting UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden creates Native American boarding school national monument to mark era of forced assimilation
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President Joe Biden designated a national monument at a former Native American boarding school in Pennsylvania on Monday to honor the resilience of Indigenous tribes whose children were forced to attend the school and hundreds of similar abusive institutions.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, KTLA 5

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa's TD pass to Jonnu Smith gives Dolphins 32-26 overtime win over Aaron Rodgers, Jets
item.description: Tagovailoa sealed it with a 10-yard touchdown pass to Jonnu Smith in overtime to help the Dolphins spoil Rodgers' first 300-yard passing game in nearly three years and beat the Jets for the ninth straight time in Miami.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden adds to the nation's list of national monuments during his term. There's an appetite for more
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt did in 1906 what Congress was unwilling to do through legislation: He used his new authority under the Antiquities Act to designate Devils Tower in Wyoming as the first national monument.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What happens when 'The Simpsons' join 'Monday Night Football'? Find out during Bengals-Cowboys
item.description: On Monday night, the entire Simpsons universe gets to experience it in a way not many could have imagined. The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5
